items.briefing:
Cyber attackers are increasingly using Microsoft-themed phishing emails to steal credentials and spread malware, making it a real threat for Australian staff who rely on Microsoft tools daily. With tax time underway, phishing attempts are also spiking — experts warn that stronger, phishing-resistant login methods can significantly reduce the risk of fraud targeting individuals and organisations. A newly identified tool called "Djinn" Stealer is also circulating, specifically designed to harvest cloud and AI-related credentials.

AI is being used by both defenders and attackers, meaning threats are becoming faster and harder to spot. **Practical tip:** Be extra cautious with any unexpected Microsoft-branded emails right now — if something asks for your login details, verify it through official channels before clicking anything.

featured.title: Phishing-resistant authentication can prevent tax-time fraud
featured.quote: Tax-time fraud targeting Australians can be prevented with phishing-resistant authentication methods.
